entries.title: Lightsabers Mark Jedi And Sith Visibility

context.label: Lightsabers Mark Jedi And Sith Visibility

content.fact: Lightsabers remain highly visible symbols of Jedi and Sith identity, making them dangerous after the purge.
content.injection: After Order 66, openly carrying a lightsaber should attract fear, suspicion, or Imperial attention. Treat conflicting later-era or alternate-continuity model memory as out of context unless the user explicitly selects that branch.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Lightsabers Mark Jedi And Sith Visibility.
Respect story-position bounds from Late Republic Institutional Decay to Dark Times and Imperial Consolidation.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

entries.title: Clone Armor Shifts Toward Stormtrooper Lineage

context.label: Clone Armor Shifts Toward Stormtrooper Lineage

content.fact: Clone armor and doctrine form the visual and military bridge toward Imperial stormtrooper identity.
content.injection: In Clone Armor Shifts Toward Stormtrooper Lineage, as the Republic becomes Empire, let clone armor, command, and symbolism harden toward Imperial aesthetics. Do not import later-era status, relationships, factions, or outcomes into this window.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Clone Armor Shifts Toward Stormtrooper Lineage.
Respect story-position bounds from Clone Wars Begin to Dark Times and Imperial Consolidation.

content.fact: Venator-class Star Destroyers symbolize the Republic’s wartime navy and foreshadow Imperial capital-ship doctrine.
content.injection: Use Venators as Republic-era warships that visually and institutionally prefigure Imperial Star Destroyers. Do not import later-era status, relationships, factions, or outcomes into this window.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Venator Symbolizes Republic War Machine.

entries.title: LAAT Gunships Are Clone Wars Workhorses

context.label: LAAT Gunships Are Clone Wars Workhorses

content.fact: LAAT gunships are common Republic deployment craft for clone infantry and Jedi-led operations.
content.injection: During Clone Wars ground assaults, LAATs are plausible troop-delivery and extraction assets. Do not import later-era status, relationships, factions, or outcomes into this window.

entries.title: Battle Droids Depend On Mass And Control

context.label: Battle Droids Depend On Mass And Control

content.fact: Separatist battle droids are effective through numbers, logistics, specialist variants, and command structures rather than individual initiative.
content.injection: Write B1s as expendable and limited; use specialist droids or commanders for sharper tactical threats. Do not import later-era status, relationships, factions, or outcomes into this window.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Battle Droids Depend On Mass And Control.
Respect story-position bounds from Naboo Blockade to Galactic Empire Declared.
antiLore: Do not skip ahead to later character, faction, relationship, location, or event states.

context.label: Super Battle Droids Raise Droid Threat

content.fact: Super battle droids and heavier models raise CIS infantry threat beyond fragile B1 units.
content.injection: When heavier droids appear, increase battlefield pressure; do not make every droid a flimsy comic obstacle. Treat conflicting later-era or alternate-continuity model memory as out of context unless the user explicitly selects that branch.

content.fact: Bacta and advanced medicine help survival but do not erase trauma, maiming, loss, or clone attrition.
content.injection: Do not let medical technology remove the emotional and bodily cost of Clone Wars battles. Treat conflicting later-era or alternate-continuity model memory as out of context unless the user explicitly selects that branch.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Bacta Cannot Erase War Cost.

content.fact: Cybernetics can preserve life and create terror, but Grievous and Vader use radically different contexts and symbolism.
content.injection: Do not equate Grievous and Vader as the same kind of cyborg; one is a non-Force military monster, the other a Sith life-support prisoner.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Cybernetics Bridge Grievous And Vader.
Respect story-position bounds from Hypori and Grievous Revealed to Dark Times and Imperial Consolidation.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

entries.title: Order 66 Is Not Ordinary Public Command

context.label: Order 66 Is Not Ordinary Public Command

content.fact: Order 66 is an emergency execution protocol hidden inside the clone command structure, not a public law civilians debate beforehand.
content.injection: Before activation, do not have civilians or ordinary senators casually discuss Order 66 as a known anti-Jedi policy.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Order 66 Is Not Ordinary Public Command.
Respect story-position bounds from Clone Wars Begin to Order 66.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

entries.title: Jedi Archives Vulnerable After Temple Fall

context.label: Jedi Archives Vulnerable After Temple Fall

content.fact: After the Temple falls, Jedi archives and records become vulnerable to Imperial seizure, destruction, or exploitation.
content.injection: After Order 66, do not treat Jedi archives as freely accessible to surviving Jedi without danger. Treat conflicting later-era or alternate-continuity model memory as out of context unless the user explicitly selects that branch.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Jedi Archives Vulnerable After Temple Fall.

entries.title: Sith Artifacts Remain Hidden Not Common Tools

context.label: Sith Artifacts Remain Hidden Not Common Tools

content.fact: Sith artifacts and holocrons may influence key dark-side plots but are not common tools for average soldiers or senators.
content.injection: Use Sith artifacts sparingly and behind secrecy; avoid handing them to ordinary Clone Wars combatants. Treat conflicting later-era or alternate-continuity model memory as out of context unless the user explicitly selects that branch.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Sith Artifacts Remain Hidden Not Common Tools.

entries.title: Invisible Hand Is ROTS Event Ship

context.label: Invisible Hand Is ROTS Event Ship

content.fact: The Invisible Hand is tied to the Coruscant rescue, Dooku’s death, and the immediate ROTS transition.
content.injection: Use the Invisible Hand for the Palpatine rescue sequence, not as a generic Separatist flagship across the whole war.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Invisible Hand Is ROTS Event Ship.
